symptoms.symptom_name: Reactive, retrospective grief tied to a recent adult neurodivergence diagnosis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distress is explicitly reactive and time-locked to an identifiable psychosocial stressor.
Reactive, event-bound sadness differs from global endogenous anhedonia — but does not exclude a co-existing depressive episode.
Grief- and rumination-driven insomnia fits an acute stress reaction.
Reduced sleep raises mania, which must be excluded by the absence of grandiosity/pressured speech/elevated mood.
Insomnia with tachycardia is a non-specific organic mimic requiring bloods.

symptoms.symptom_name: Markedly reduced food and fluid intake for 48 hours (acute self-neglect)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Near-absent intake over 48h drives hypovolaemia and metabolic strain.
Self-care collapse is a consequence of the acute crisis but is itself an independent physiological risk.
Rumination re-contextualising the past through a new diagnostic lens fits the adjustment picture.
Thoughts are logical and reality-based, not delusional or disorganised.

symptoms.symptom_name: Complete functional collapse — bedbound, not working, unable to self-care

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severity of functional impairment marks the crisis as acute and warrants urgent assessment.
Tachycardia is consistent with hypovolaemia from reduced intake.
A measured resting tachycardia mandates exclusion of an organic driver by bloods; sealed clinician measurement — the AI infers cardiovascular strain only from the patient's reported palpitations.

symptoms.symptom_name: Flattened affect, poor eye contact, delayed cognitive processing (clinician-observed)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Consistent with exhaustion/unmasking burnout; overlaps with depression and so is non-discriminating alone.
Psychomotor slowing and flat affect are shared features; require MSE to interpret.

symptoms.symptom_name: Subjective palpitations (heart racing/pounding) reported by patient

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patient-reportable proxy for the sealed measured tachycardia; admissible in telehealth but unverified.
Orthostatic symptoms suggest volume depletion significant enough to lower the threshold for ED.

symptoms.symptom_name: Suicidal ideation / thoughts of not wanting to be alive (gated; no stated plan)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Major depressive episode with suicidal ideation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Passive death wishes with hopeless exhaustion mandate a formal in-person risk assessment; cannot be excluded remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No hallucinations, delusions, grandiosity or elevated mood (pertinent negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of psychotic features makes acute psychosis very unlikely.
No grandiosity/pressured speech/elevated mood argues strongly against a manic episode.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Insomnia + near-absent intake + resting tachycardia + postural symptoms together mark evolving physiological decompensation that outranks the psychosocial label for triage.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person mental state examination and structured suicide-risk assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The only reliable way to exclude a major depressive episode with active suicidal intent and to assure immediate safety — cannot be done via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Vital signs including lying and standing blood pressure
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the tachycardia and quantifies orthostatic drop / hydration status.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urea, electrolytes and creatinine (U&E) with hydration assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses dehydration and acute kidney injury from 48h of reduced intake.
Excludes thyrotoxicosis as an organic mimic of tachycardia, insomnia and agitation.
No focal neurology or cardiac red flags beyond sinus tachycardia; broad imaging would be over-investigation and is not indicated at this stage.
